creator: yuripv@yuripv.net
text:
Originally, it was a request on IRC asking how to *programmatically* find the original name of renamed interface as some tunneling software wanted the tun device, not the newly named interface.

I understand that it's not a usual use case, but in any case seemed helpful enough to implement (I didn't find any other way) -- if it isn't or there are smarter/easier ways to find the original name, feel free to reject this.

The output now looks like the following (with devname listed only for renamed interface):

# ifconfig tun0 create name foo
foo
# ifconfig
vmx0: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=60039b<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,TSO6,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6>
        ether 00:50:56:00:00:34
        inet6 fe80::250:56ff:fe00:34%vmx0 prefixlen 64 scopeid 0x1
        inet 192.168.1.34 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 192.168.1.255
        nd6 options=23<PERFORMNUD,ACCEPT_RTADV,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        media: Ethernet autoselect
        status: active
lo0: flags=8049<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 16384
        options=600003<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6>
        inet6 ::1 prefixlen 128
        inet6 fe80::1%lo0 prefixlen 64 scopeid 0x2
        inet 127.0.0.1 netmask 0xff000000
        nd6 options=21<PERFORMNUD,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        groups: lo
foo: flags=8010<POINTOPOINT,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        devname: tun0
        options=80000<LINKSTATE>
        nd6 options=21<PERFORMNUD,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        groups: tun
